    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/api/admin.go", "symbol": "getAuthorizedSettings"}
  ],
  "text": "Grafana's configuration system is implemented across multiple Go source files in the pkg/setting directory, with API endpoints in pkg/api. The system consists of three main components:\n\n1. **Configuration Parsing and Storage** (pkg/setting/setting.go):\n   - The `Cfg` struct (line 83) serves as the central configuration container storing all parsed settings\n   - `parseINIFile()` (line 1165) is the main INI parser that reads and processes configuration files using the gopkg.in/ini.v1 library\n   - The function parses the INI file structure and delegates section-specific parsing to helper functions like `readServerSettings()` (line 1919), `readAuthSettings()` (line 1696), and many others for specific configuration domains\n   - `valueAsString()` (line 1453) is a helper function that retrieves configuration values with default fallbacks\n\n2. **Environment Variable Override Chain** (pkg/setting/setting.go and pkg/setting/expanders.go):\n   - `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` (line 744) applies environment variable overrides to configuration values\n   - `EnvKey()` (line 855) generates the environment variable name from section and key names by converting to uppercase and replacing dots with underscores (e.g., \"database.name\" becomes \"GF_DATABASE_NAME\")\n   - `expandConfig()` (pkg/setting/expanders.go:52) processes configuration values containing placeholders like `${VAR_NAME}`\n   - The system supports two types of expanders: `envExpander` (line 114 in expanders.go) for environment variables and `fileExpander` (line 132) for file content references\n   - `applyCommandLineProperties()` (line 878) allows command-line arguments to override configuration settings\n\n3. **Sensitive Value Masking** (pkg/setting/setting.go):\n   - `RedactedValue()` (line 660) masks sensitive configuration values before exposure\n   - The function uses pattern matching to identify sensitive keys containing patterns like \"PASSWORD\", \"SECRET\", \"PRIVATE_KEY\", \"CLIENT_SECRET\", \"TOKEN\", etc. (lines 667-688)\n   - It returns the constant `RedactedPassword` (\"*********\") for matching keys\n   - `RedactedURL()` (line 711) handles masking of credentials in URLs by using the standard url.URL.Redacted() method\n   - Exceptions are made for telemetry-related keys like \"RUDDERSTACK\", \"APPLICATION_INSIGHTS\", and \"SENTRY\" which are not masked\n\n4. **Configuration Provider Interface** (pkg/setting/provider.go):\n   - The `Provider` interface (line 36) provides thread-safe access to configuration\n   - `OSSImpl` struct (line 114) implements Provider for open-source Grafana\n   - `Current()` method (line 118) returns a `SettingsBag` (type definition at line 97) - a nested map structure containing all configuration sections and their key-value pairs\n   - Values in the SettingsBag are automatically redacted by calling `RedactedValue()` during population (line 124)\n   - `VerboseSettingsBag` (line 101) is an extended type that tracks the source of each configuration value (database, system, etc.)\n\n5. **Admin Settings Endpoint** (pkg/api/admin.go):\n   - `AdminGetSettings()` (line 29) is the HTTP handler for the `/api/admin/settings` endpoint that returns current configuration\n   - `getAuthorizedSettings()` (line 77) filters configuration based on user permissions, enforcing access control\n   - The function checks permissions using `ac.ActionSettingsRead` with scopes for different configuration sections\n   - Only authorized settings are returned to the user\n   - `getAuthorizedVerboseSettings()` (line 118) provides verbose settings with source information while respecting authorization\n\n6. **Default Values System**:\n   - Default values for configuration options are set throughout the setting_*.go files (e.g., setting_anonymous.go, setting_jwt.go)\n   - Section-specific reader functions use ini.Key methods like `MustString()`, `MustBool()`, `MustInt()`, and `MustDuration()` to provide type conversion with defaults\n   - For example, in setting_anonymous.go line 15, `MustBool(false)` provides false as default for the enabled flag\n   - The configuration system supports approximately 40+ setting files organized by feature domain (auth, storage, alerting, plugins, etc.)\n\nThe complete configuration loading chain follows this sequence:\n1. `NewCfg()` creates a Cfg instance\n2. `loadConfigFile()` reads the INI file\n3. `parseINIFile()` parses the INI structure and delegates to section-specific parsers\n4. `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` applies GF_* environment variable overrides\n5. `applyCommandLineProperties()` applies cfg:* command-line argument overrides\n6. `expandConfig()` expands placeholders in values\n7. `ProvideProvider()` creates a Provider instance wrapping the Cfg\n8. `Current()` returns a SettingsBag with values automatically masked via `RedactedValue()`\n9. `AdminGetSettings()` in the API handler calls `getAuthorizedSettings()` to filter and return authorized settings\n\nThis architecture ensures secure configuration management with proper masking of sensitive values, flexible override mechanisms, and fine-grained access control through the admin API."
}

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Grafana Configuration File Parsing and Override Chain

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement configuration loading: the INI file parser, the environment variable override logic, the default values per section, and the sensitive config value masking for the /api/admin/settings endpoint.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the platform domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement the configuration loading system. Let me start by discovering the repository structure and searching for the key components.
Now I'll create a comprehensive analysis file with all the findings about Grafana's configuration system.

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of Grafana's configuration loading system. Here's what I found:

## Summary

I discovered **14 primary source files** and **20+ key symbols** that implement Grafana's configuration system:

### **Core Components:**

1. **INI Parser** (`pkg/setting/setting.go`):
   - `parseINIFile()` - Main parser using gopkg.in/ini.v1
   - `Cfg` struct - Central configuration container
   - Delegates to 40+ setting_*.go files for domain-specific parsing

2. **Environment Variable Override Chain**:
   - `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` - Applies GF_* environment variables
   - `EnvKey()` - Converts config keys to env var names (e.g., database.name → GF_DATABASE_NAME)
   - `expandConfig()` - Expands placeholders like `${VAR_NAME}`
   - Supports `envExpander` and `fileExpander` for content substitution

3. **Sensitive Value Masking**:
   - `RedactedValue()` - Masks passwords, secrets, tokens, private keys, etc.
   - `RedactedURL()` - Masks credentials in URLs
   - Returns `"*********"` for sensitive patterns
   - Exceptions: RUDDERSTACK, APPLICATION_INSIGHTS, SENTRY remain unmasked

4. **Configuration Provider** (`pkg/setting/provider.go`):
   - `Provider` interface - Thread-safe configuration access
   - `OSSImpl.Current()` - Returns `SettingsBag` with automatically redacted values
   - `VerboseSettingsBag` - Tracks configuration source (database/system)

5. **Admin API** (`pkg/api/admin.go`):
   - `AdminGetSettings()` - HTTP endpoint for `/api/admin/settings`
   - `getAuthorizedSettings()` - Enforces access control per section/key
   - `getAuthorizedVerboseSettings()` - Verbose variant with sources

### **Configuration Load Sequence:**
INI parsing → Env var overrides → Command-line properties → Placeholder expansion → Provider wrapping → Admin API access

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with all files, symbols, call chains, and detailed narrative explanation.

arguments.query: repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec$ file:pkg INI configuration parsing environment variables
repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec$ file:pkg/api sensitive mask settings secret password

`VerboseSettingsBag` (line 101) is an extended type that tracks the source of each configuration value (database, system, etc.)\n\n5. **Admin Settings Endpoint** (pkg/api/admin.go):\n   - `AdminGetSettings()` (line 29) is the HTTP handler for the `/api/admin/settings` endpoint that returns current configuration\n   - `getAuthorizedSettings()` (line 77) filters configuration based on user permissions, enforcing access control\n   - The function checks permissions using `ac.ActionSettingsRead` with scopes for different configuration sections\n   - Only authorized settings are returned to the user\n   - `getAuthorizedVerboseSettings()` (line 118) provides verbose settings with source information while respecting authorization\n\n6. **Default Values System**:\n   - Default values for configuration options are set throughout the setting_*.go files (e.g., setting_anonymous.go, setting_jwt.go)\n   - Section-specific reader functions use ini.Key methods like `MustString()`, `MustBool()`, `MustInt()`, and `MustDuration()` to provide type conversion with defaults\n   - For example, in setting_anonymous.go line 15, `MustBool(false)` provides false as default for the enabled flag\n   - The configuration system supports approximately 40+ setting files organized by feature domain (auth, storage, alerting, plugins, etc.)\n\nThe complete configuration loading chain follows this sequence:\n1. `NewCfg()` creates a Cfg instance\n2. `loadConfigFile()` reads the INI file\n3. `parseINIFile()` parses the INI structure and delegates to section-specific parsers\n4. `applyEnvVariableOverrides()` applies GF_* environment variable overrides\n5. `applyCommandLineProperties()` applies cfg:* command-line argument overrides\n6. `expandConfig()` expands placeholders in values\n7. `ProvideProvider()` creates a Provider instance wrapping the Cfg\n8. `Current()` returns a SettingsBag with values automatically masked via `RedactedValue()`\n9. `AdminGetSettings()` in the API handler calls `getAuthorizedSettings()` to filter and return authorized settings\n\nThis architecture ensures secure configuration management with proper masking of sensitive values, flexible override mechanisms, and fine-grained access control through the admin API."
}
